events_calendar: Time Out LA Things to Do

restaurant_openings.name: Chef Joshua Gil, a Pioneer of Los Angeles’s Alta California Movement, Dies at 50
restaurant_openings.summary: On June 22, chef Joshua Misael Gil, a pioneering chef of Alta California cuisine who was known in Los Angeles as a founder of the Supper Liberation Front, Tacos Punta Cabras, Mírame, and Mírate, passed away after a nearly four-year battle with cancer. Gil was 50 years old. The chef maintained a deep passion for […]

restaurant_openings.name: A ‘Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles’ Pizzeria Skates Into Santa Monica With $54 Pies
restaurant_openings.summary: The first official Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles pizzeria just skated onto Santa Monica’s Third Street Promenade with a neon-drenched retro dining room, full bar, and New York–style slices. The pizzeria, which held its grand opening on Saturday, June 20, offers a menu from veteran pizzaiolo Angelo Womack, who previously owned Oak & Rye in San […]

restaurant_openings.name: All the New Restaurant Openings to Know in Los Angeles
restaurant_openings.summary: Los Angeles is no stranger to restaurant openings, whether debuts in iconic buildings helmed by big-name chefs, humble neighborhood haunts, or pop-ups leaping into permanent spaces. The city’s dining scene continues to grow even as operators face stunning challenges related to ongoing impacts from the 2020 coronavirus pandemic, the 2023 Hollywood labor strikes, the 2025 Palisades and […]

restaurant_openings.name: Olvera Street
restaurant_openings.summary: Olvera Street.’ Historic L.A. restaurants on brink of closure As Boyle Heights fire still burns, restaurants depend on which way the wind blows Tiki kitsch, rooftop views and amaro cocktails, L.A.’s new bar scene has it all For Subscribers Food is L.A.’s love language. Here are the 50 dining experiences that define living here For Subscribers 7 new rooftops for soaking up the L.A. sun this summer

restaurant_openings.name: Suki
restaurant_openings.summary: A Beverly Hills handroll bar from the team behind Savta and Sora. It features a long counter where you can enjoy fresh sushi and handrolls.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: Beverly Hills

restaurant_openings.name: Clio Vine
restaurant_openings.summary: A Beverly Grove cafe serving coffee, matcha, and pastries from Artelice. They are also known for their smoothie menu featuring superfood ingredients.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: Beverly Grove

restaurant_openings.name: Bengara
restaurant_openings.summary: A Japanese spot in the Arts District that explores meat-on-a-stick and other traditional dishes. It features a thorough menu highlighting various grilled skewers.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: Arts District

restaurant_openings.name: Nam Coffee
restaurant_openings.summary: A local roaster importing robusta beans from Vietnam, which just opened its second cafe location in Chinatown. It is a good spot for something sweet or getting work done.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: Chinatown

restaurant_openings.name: Korean Ice Co.
restaurant_openings.summary: A new Korean dessert spot in Sawtelle. It offers a variety of ice cream and sweet treats in a busy neighborhood.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: Sawtelle

restaurant_openings.name: Coucou
restaurant_openings.summary: A French-ish bistro in Manhattan Beach perfect for steak frites and oyster platters. It is a great spot for Francophiles and martini drinkers in the South Bay.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: Manhattan Beach

restaurant_openings.name: Shin-Sen-Gumi Yakitori Izakaya
restaurant_openings.summary: A new location of the Shin-Sen-Gumi chain in Little Tokyo focusing on yakitori and izakaya dishes. Menu items include chicken gizzard skewers and pan-fried gyoza.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: Little Tokyo

restaurant_openings.name: Big Sur Grill
restaurant_openings.summary: A Santa Monica dinner spot offering live-fire, farm-to-table cooking. It promises a menu focused on fresh ingredients and open-flame techniques.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: Santa Monica

restaurant_openings.name: Dr. Bombay Ice Cream
restaurant_openings.summary: Snoop Dogg’s latest side quest is an ice cream shop in Venice. It offers a selection of cold treats right on Ocean Front Walk.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: Venice

restaurant_openings.name: Luscious Dumplings
restaurant_openings.summary: The popular SGV dumpling empire has opened a new branch in Beverly Hills. It is located just a short walk from the central shopping area.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: Beverly Hills

restaurant_openings.name: Malaya
restaurant_openings.summary: A popular Silver Lake cafe that has expanded with a new location in Arcadia. They serve Filipino-inspired drinks and rainbow-colored beverages.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: Arcadia

restaurant_openings.name: Bar Betsy
restaurant_openings.summary: A new wine bar in Altadena located next to its sibling restaurant, Betsy. It focuses on drinks and light bites in a cozy neighborhood setting.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: Altadena

restaurant_openings.name: Mitsi
restaurant_openings.summary: A new sushi bar and cocktail spot north of Chinatown, opened by a member of Mumford & Sons. It is perfect for great cocktails and a light bite.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: Downtown LA

restaurant_openings.name: Joint Seafood & Uoichiba
restaurant_openings.summary: A fish market known for dry-aging fish that has now opened a location in Downtown LA. It offers high-quality seafood and a unique dry-aging process.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: Downtown LA

restaurant_openings.name: Très LA Bistro
restaurant_openings.summary: A new bistro located in the Pacific Design Center in West Hollywood. It serves as a cafe and dining spot for those visiting the surrounding showrooms and offices.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: West Hollywood

restaurant_openings.name: Perse
restaurant_openings.summary: The family behind one of Los Angeles’s best Persian restaurants, Toranj, opened a new, upscale destination for wagyu kebabs, tart fesenjoon, and sour cherry–dotted rice in February. Set on San Vicente Boulevard in Brentwood, Perse offers regional dishes and shareable grilled items like koobideh and saffron lamb chops.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: 11677 West San Vicente Boulevard, STE 315, Los Angeles, California 90049, United States

restaurant_openings.name: Badmaash Venice
restaurant_openings.summary: Acclaimed modern Indian restaurant Badmaash expanded with a new location on Abbot Kinney in Venice on Friday, March 27. The restaurant serves a menu of familiar classics alongside newer dishes like steak frites and hamachi crudo in a warmly lit space.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: 1600 Abbot Kinney Boulevard, Venice, California 90291, United States

restaurant_openings.name: Very Thai
restaurant_openings.summary: Taiwan-based Thai restaurant, Very Thai, made its U.S. debut at Westfield Century City in mid-March. The restaurant serves traditional Thai cooking like Massaman beef curry alongside unexpected renditions like green curry pad Thai.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: 10250 Santa Monica Boulevard, STE 2800, Los Angeles, California 90067, United States

restaurant_openings.name: Bad Roman
restaurant_openings.summary: New York’s irreverent, maximalist Italian destination, Bad Roman, recently made its Los Angeles debut in Beverly Hills. The menu includes roasted garlic babka, a seafood tower, and various fresh pastas made daily at the restaurant.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: 267 North Canon Drive, Beverly Hills, California 90210, United States

restaurant_openings.name: Lielle
restaurant_openings.summary: Celebrated Swedish chef Marcus Jernmark debuted a fresh California-centric restaurant in the former Pico-Robertson Bicyclette space on February 12. The 42-seat venue showcases a simple four-course menu designed to shift with the seasons.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: 9575 West Pico Boulevard, Los Angeles, California 90035, United States

restaurant_openings.name: Sushi Samba West Hollywood
restaurant_openings.summary: London-based Sushi Samba has landed in West Hollywood, serving a blend of Japanese, Brazilian, and Peruvian flavors. The lively restaurant features a menu of sashimi, sushi, ceviches, and robata-grilled items accompanied by live entertainment.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: 639 North La Peer Drive, West Hollywood, California 90069, United States

restaurant_openings.name: Sushi Nakazawa
restaurant_openings.summary: Chef Daisuke Nakazawa has opened a Los Angeles outpost of his Michelin-starred temple of sushi on South Robertson Boulevard. The restaurant offers signature Edomae-style nigiri-centered omakase alongside an expanded chef’s menu.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: 145 S Robertson Blvd, Los Angeles, CA 90048

restaurant_openings.name: Picala
restaurant_openings.summary: Acme Hospitality made its Los Angeles debut with Picala, a new restaurant in West Adams that embodies California cooking through a Spanish lens. The menu features seasonal shaved asparagus salad, pan con tomate, and prime rib-eye with Spanish accents.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: 3321 South La Cienega Boulevard, STE G, Los Angeles, California 90016, United States

restaurant_openings.name: Alta Adams
restaurant_openings.summary: Alta Adams is back with a new seasonal menu from chef and owner Keith Corbin featuring citrusy hamachi crudo and fire-roasted cabbage. The restaurant continues its focus on California soul food with updated takes on traditional flavors.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: 5359 West Adams Boulevard, Los Angeles, California 90016, United States

restaurant_openings.name: Jacaranda
restaurant_openings.summary: Chef Daniel Patterson and Sarah Lewitinn debuted a 10-course tasting menu that embraces fine dining in a casual, intimate 30-seat room. The menu focuses heavily on seasonal California vegetables and high-quality local seafood.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: 6623 Melrose Avenue, Los Angeles, California 90038, United States

restaurant_openings.name: ABL Hollywood
restaurant_openings.summary: The team behind Little Tokyo’s A Beautiful Life opened a new destination for Jamaican-Chinese cooking in Hollywood. The menu features oxtail macaroni and cheese, jerk fried egg rolls, and whole escovitch snapper.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: 1649 North Cahuenga Boulevard, Los Angeles, California 90028, United States

restaurant_openings.name: Roshona Bilash
restaurant_openings.summary: Roshona Bilash is a family-run Bangladeshi spot in Melrose Hill serving comforting platters of goat biryani and kebabs. Special highlights include the chile and ginger-tinged nihari stew and weekend-only braised lamb shank.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: 861 N Western Ave, Los Angeles, CA, 90012, US

restaurant_openings.name: Bar di Bello
restaurant_openings.summary: Bar di Bello is a Milan-inspired bar and restaurant located at Silver Lake’s Sunset Row development. The menu features sophisticated cocktails alongside Italian mains like trofie alla Genovese and chicken cutlet Milanese.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: 3300 West Sunset Boulevard, #109-110, Los Angeles, California 90026, United States

restaurant_openings.name: The Holloway
restaurant_openings.summary: The Holloway sports bar has reopened in Echo Park with easygoing cocktails and a menu of casual bites. The revived neighborhood hangout features televisions for sports fans and classic bar food like burgers and hot dogs.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: 1320 Echo Park Avenue, Los Angeles, California 90026, United States

restaurant_openings.name: Bengara
restaurant_openings.summary: Bengara in the Arts District serves a menu centered on Japan’s binchotan charcoal used for genshiyaki-style cooking. Featured dishes include grilled rice skewers with uni and pork shoulder with house bacon and fermented garlic.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: 803 Traction Avenue, STE 140, Los Angeles, California 90013, United States

events_calendar.summary: Saturday in Los Angeles? Let’s party! In the queue for today in L.A. you’ll find the LACMA Art Parade & The post 10 Things To Do For This Saturday in L.A. [6-20-2026] appeared first on WE LIKE L.A. .

events_calendar.name: Best things to do this weekend in LA and SoCal: June 19-21
events_calendar.summary: Juneteenth events, Father’s Day treats, LACMA’s Art Parade, River Solstice Festival and more of the best things to do this weekend.

events_calendar.name: Things To Do This Week in Los Angeles [6-8-2026 to 6-12-2026]
events_calendar.summary: Brace for impact. World Cup fever is coming to Los Angeles. As the beautiful game’s biggest tournament enters the frame, The post Things To Do This Week in Los Angeles [6-8-2026 to 6-12-2026] appeared first on WE LIKE L.A. .

events_calendar.name: Hospital of Emotions
events_calendar.summary: A visually dazzling, site-specific art pop-up has taken over the former St. Vincent Medical Center featuring 80 individual hospital rooms transformed by 70 local and international artists.

source.name: Time Out LA Things to Do

events_calendar.name: CicLAvia—Leimert Park meets Expo Park
events_calendar.summary: This massive open streets event allows bike-riders and pedestrians to take over the road and explore neighborhoods.

source.name: Time Out LA Things to Do

events_calendar.name: Griffith Park Free Shakespeare Festival
events_calendar.summary: Outdoor performances of Shakespeare's works held at the historic Old Zoo in Griffith Park.

source.name: Time Out LA Things to Do

events_calendar.name: Yoko Ono: Music of the Mind
events_calendar.summary: A comprehensive exhibition at the Broad focusing on the artist, musician and activist Yoko Ono.

source.name: Time Out LA Things to Do

events_calendar.name: Thursday Jazz Night Series at the Spotlight
events_calendar.summary: A weekly jazz series at the Spotlight, a popular nightlife destination in Hollywood.

source.name: Time Out LA Things to Do

events_calendar.name: Friday Movie Nights
events_calendar.summary: A free series of outdoor movie screenings held at the former Hollywood & Highland.

source.name: Time Out LA Things to Do

events_calendar.name: Marilyn Monroe: Hollywood Icon
events_calendar.summary: An exhibition at the Academy Museum celebrating Marilyn Monroe's 100th birthday with iconic artifacts from her career.

source.name: Time Out LA Things to Do

events_calendar.name: The Music Center’s Dance DTLA
events_calendar.summary: A free outdoor event in Downtown L.A. featuring various music genres and guided dance lessons.

source.name: Time Out LA Things to Do

events_calendar.name: Summer Music Series at the Original Farmers Market
events_calendar.summary: A series of free, family-friendly outdoor concerts held at the historic Original Farmers Market.

source.name: Time Out LA Things to Do

events_calendar.name: Forest Bathing at the Arboretum
events_calendar.summary: Guided walks at the Los Angeles County Arboretum based on the Japanese practice of forest bathing.

source.name: Time Out LA Things to Do

events_calendar.name: The Music Center’s ¡DALE! Fútbol Watch Party: Spain v. Uruguay
events_calendar.summary: A public event in Downtown L.A. to watch the soccer match between Spain and Uruguay.

source.name: Time Out LA Things to Do

events_calendar.name: Inventing America: The Comic Book Revolution
events_calendar.summary: An exhibition at the Skirball Cultural Center exploring the cultural impact and history of comic books in America.

source.name: Time Out LA Things to Do

events_calendar.name: The Queer Mercado
events_calendar.summary: A marketplace dedicated to queer Latino creators and vendors at the birthplace of Los Angeles.

source.name: Time Out LA Things to Do

events_calendar.name: Declarations of Independence
events_calendar.summary: A Pride Month concert series from the Gay Men’s Chorus of Los Angeles.

source.name: Time Out LA Things to Do

events_calendar.name: Beach Movie Nights
events_calendar.summary: Free movie screenings on the sand at Dockweiler Beach.

source.name: Time Out LA Things to Do

events_calendar.name: Experience Play at Union Station
events_calendar.summary: An immersive experience called Play hosted at the historic Los Angeles Union Station. It offers an engaging environment for visitors to explore and enjoy during the summer months.

events_calendar.name: Grand Central Market-Downtown Los Angeles-FIFA Store-Merchandise
events_calendar.summary: Official FIFA World Cup 2026 merchandise available at the Grand Central Market in Downtown Los Angeles. Fans can purchase official gear and memorabilia at this central location.

events_calendar.name: Bitter Root Pottery Aqueous
events_calendar.summary: An aqueous-themed pottery event or exhibit held at Bitter Root Pottery. Participants can view or engage with ceramic works inspired by water and fluid forms.

events_calendar.name: VISIT THE IN-PROCESS PALISADES FIRE MEMORIAL AND HEAR THE STORIES OF THE STONEHENGE BY THE SEA
events_calendar.summary: A guided tour of the in-progress Palisades Fire Memorial featuring stories of the Stonehenge by the Sea. The memorial commemorates local history and significant fire events.

events_calendar.name: 60out Escape Rooms - Koreatown
events_calendar.summary: Immersive escape room experiences located in the Koreatown district of Los Angeles. Groups can challenge themselves with various themed puzzles and interactive storylines.

events_calendar.name: Play Fan v Fan Soccer matches. Represent your nation.
events_calendar.summary: Fans can participate in soccer matches representing their nations at SoFive SouthGate. This event fosters community spirit and friendly competition among international football enthusiasts.

events_calendar.name: Santa Monica-FIFA Store- Merchandise
events_calendar.summary: Official FIFA World Cup 2026 merchandise store located at the 3rd Street Promenade in Santa Monica. It offers a variety of products for fans to commemorate the global tournament.

events_calendar.name: Manhattan Beach-FIFA Store-Merchandise
events_calendar.summary: Official FIFA World Cup 2026 merchandise available at Manhattan Village in Manhattan Beach. This location provides access to exclusive tournament gear and apparel.

events_calendar.name: Hollywood-FIFA Store-Merchandise
events_calendar.summary: Official FIFA World Cup 2026 merchandise available at Ovation Hollywood in the heart of Hollywood. Fans can browse and buy tournament-themed clothing and accessories.

events_calendar.name: Century City Westfield
events_calendar.summary: Community viewing parties for World Cup matches and events at Westfield Century City. The mall provides a vibrant atmosphere for fans to watch games together on large screens.
events_calendar.venue_or_address: Holiday Market at Century City Mall

events_calendar.name: GAME ON! Science, Sports & Play
events_calendar.summary: An exhibit exploring the science behind sports and play at the California Science Center. It features interactive displays that explain the physics and biology of athletic performance.

events_calendar.name: Downtown Los Angeles-FIFA Store-Merchandise
events_calendar.summary: Official FIFA World Cup 2026 merchandise available at The Bloc in Downtown Los Angeles. This retail destination hosts a dedicated store for all tournament-related merchandise.

events_calendar.name: Los Angeles World Cup 26 Fan Zone at Hansen Dam Lake
events_calendar.summary: An official fan zone for the FIFA World Cup 2026 located at Hansen Dam Lake. It features public viewings of matches, entertainment, and family-friendly activities.

events_calendar.name: Round of 32 – Group 1H vs 2J
events_calendar.summary: A FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 32 match between the winner of Group H and the runner-up of Group J. The game takes place at the Los Angeles Stadium.

events_calendar.name: Soccer Celebration - LA Galaxy
events_calendar.summary: A community soccer celebration hosted by the LA Galaxy at Dignity Health Sports Park. The event includes fan activities, viewing parties, and celebrations of local soccer culture.
